decisions.decision: dBN framework fully mapped — every path hits Montgomery or circularity
decisions.rationale: Log-concavity is RH in disguise, collision times need gap distribution (Montgomery), energy bound is too crude, Euler product controlled only 39% of S_N

decisions.decision: Pivot to Jacobi matrix approach (constructive Hilbert-Polya)
decisions.rationale: Genuinely new angle. Jacobi parameters capture arithmetic structure 50x above GUE noise. Connects to primes via moments and explicit formula. Self-adjointness of infinite Jacobi matrix would imply RH

decisions.decision: Collision time is t=-g0^2/8, not g0^2/4 (Session 15 error corrected)
decisions.rationale: 2-body ODE dg/dt=+4/g gives g^2=g0^2+8t. Alpha correction is ~5-10%, not ~88%

next_action: Continue Jacobi matrix investigation. Priority: (1) unfold zeros properly, (2) stabilize numerics with full reorthogonalization, (3) look for prime signature in b_k fluctuations, (4) derive moment-to-Jacobi connection analytically via explicit formula.
context_notes: Session 16 was the deepest analytical session yet. Systematically explored and CLOSED four dBN proof paths (collision, log-concavity, energy, Euler product). Each barrier is structural, not computational. The session ended with a pivot to the Jacobi matrix approach — extracting the tridiagonal operator whose eigenvalues are zeta zeros. First computation shows strong arithmetic signal (50x GUE noise). This is the constructive Hilbert-Polya program made concrete. Environment: /c/Python313/python.exe, matplotlib Agg backend.
